LABEL: Airway compromise
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: Call 911 if stridor present; Do not lay patient flat; NPO
RATIONALE: Stridor or inability to swallow saliva suggests epiglottitis or deep space infection

LABEL: Peritonsillar abscess signs
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: Urgent ENT evaluation; CT neck with contrast; IV antibiotics
RATIONALE: Muffled voice + trismus strongly suggests peritonsillar abscess

LABEL: Significant neck swelling
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: Urgent evaluation for deep space infection; CT neck
RATIONALE: Neck swelling with fever suggests deep space infection

LABEL: Respiratory distress
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: Assess oxygen saturation; Consider chest X-ray
RATIONALE: Dyspnea with pharyngitis needs workup

LABEL: Immunocompromised patient
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: Lower threshold for testing and antibiotics; Consider broader differential
RATIONALE: Immunocompromised patients need more aggressive workup

LABEL: Persistent symptoms > 10 days
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: Consider mono test; CBC; Consider ENT referral
RATIONALE: Persistent pharyngitis beyond viral window needs evaluation

LABEL: Possible scarlet fever
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: Rapid strep test; Consider scarlet fever treatment
RATIONALE: Rash + fever + sore throat = possible scarlet fever

LABEL: Mastoiditis signs
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: Urgent CT temporal bone; IV antibiotics; ENT consult stat
RATIONALE: Mastoid tenderness + fever suggests acute mastoiditis requiring emergent evaluation

LABEL: Mastoid pain with severe symptoms
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: Urgent ENT evaluation; CT temporal bone; Pain management
RATIONALE: Severe mastoid pain without fever still requires urgent workup

LABEL: Facial nerve involvement
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: Urgent ENT evaluation; CT/MRI temporal bone; Consider Ramsay Hunt syndrome; Start antivirals if vesicles present
RATIONALE: Facial weakness with ear pain suggests facial nerve involvement — emergent evaluation required

LABEL: Neurological symptoms
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: Urgent audiogram; MRI IAC; Neurology consult
RATIONALE: Vertigo/sudden hearing loss with ear pain suggests inner ear or central pathology

LABEL: Ear trauma with drainage
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: Keep ear dry; ENT evaluation within 24h; Do not place anything in ear canal
RATIONALE: Trauma with drainage may indicate TM perforation

LABEL: Ear trauma with neuro signs
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: CT temporal bone; Neurology consult; Urgent ENT
RATIONALE: Trauma + neuro symptoms suggests temporal bone fracture

LABEL: Neck swelling with fever
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: CT neck with contrast; Consider deep space infection; ENT evaluation
RATIONALE: Neck swelling with fever and ear pain suggests parotid/deep space infection

LABEL: Immunocompromised patient
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: Lower threshold for imaging and antibiotics; Consider malignant otitis externa
RATIONALE: Immunocompromised patients with ear pain need broader workup

LABEL: PE risk - SOB + chest pain
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: CT pulmonary angiography stat; D-dimer; Oxygen monitoring; Anticoagulation if confirmed
RATIONALE: SOB + chest pain with cough raises high suspicion for pulmonary embolism

LABEL: PE risk - chest pain + DVT signs
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: CT pulmonary angiography; Lower extremity duplex ultrasound; D-dimer; Anticoagulation if confirmed
RATIONALE: Chest pain with DVT risk factors (leg swelling or recent immobility/surgery) requires emergent PE workup

LABEL: Chest pain with cough
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: Chest X-ray; ECG; Consider troponin; Outpatient CTA if Wells score elevated
RATIONALE: Chest pain with persistent cough needs urgent cardiac and pulmonary evaluation

LABEL: Hemoptysis
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: CT chest with contrast; CBC; Coagulation studies; ENT/Pulm consult
RATIONALE: Hemoptysis requires emergent evaluation to rule out PE, malignancy, or pulmonary hemorrhage

LABEL: Hypoxemia
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: Supplemental oxygen; ABG; Chest X-ray; CT if needed; Admit for monitoring
RATIONALE: Low oxygen saturation with cough requires emergent evaluation and oxygen support

LABEL: SOB without chest pain
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: Chest X-ray; PFTs; Consider CT chest; Pulse oximetry
RATIONALE: Shortness of breath with persistent cough warrants urgent pulmonary evaluation

LABEL: ACS pattern
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: ECG stat; Troponin; Aspirin 325mg; Activate cath lab if STEMI; Oxygen if SpO2 < 94%
RATIONALE: Exertional chest pain with radiation or diaphoresis is classic ACS presentation

LABEL: Pulmonary embolism risk
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: CT pulmonary angiography stat; D-dimer; Lower extremity duplex; Anticoagulation if confirmed
RATIONALE: Pleuritic chest pain + SOB + VTE risk factors requires emergent PE evaluation

LABEL: Aortic dissection pattern
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: CT angiography chest/abdomen/pelvis stat; BP both arms; Emergent surgical consult; IV labetalol
RATIONALE: Tearing pain with neuro symptoms or radiation suggests aortic dissection

LABEL: Syncope with chest pain
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: ECG stat; Troponin; Echocardiogram; Telemetry; Consider CT head
RATIONALE: Syncope with chest pain suggests dangerous arrhythmia or hemodynamic compromise

LABEL: Hypertensive emergency
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: IV antihypertensives; ECG; Troponin; CT head if neuro symptoms; Renal function
RATIONALE: Chest pain with hypertensive emergency symptoms requires immediate BP control

LABEL: SOB without other danger
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: ECG; Chest X-ray; Pulse oximetry; D-dimer if PE risk; BNP if CHF suspected
RATIONALE: SOB with chest pain warrants urgent cardiac and pulmonary evaluation

LABEL: Stroke signs
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: Activate stroke protocol; CT head stat; MRI if available; Neurology consult; Check blood glucose
RATIONALE: Focal neurological deficits with dizziness suggest stroke

LABEL: Posterior circulation stroke
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: MRI brain with DWI stat; CTA head/neck; Neurology consult; Admit for monitoring
RATIONALE: Diplopia + severe gait instability suggests posterior circulation stroke

LABEL: Cardiac syncope
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: ECG stat; Telemetry; Troponin; Echocardiogram; Electrolytes
RATIONALE: Syncope with palpitations suggests dangerous arrhythmia

LABEL: Meningitis signs
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: CT head; Lumbar puncture; Blood cultures; Empiric IV antibiotics stat
RATIONALE: Severe headache + neck stiffness + dizziness suggests meningitis or SAH

LABEL: GI bleed with dizziness
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: CBC stat; Type and screen; IV access x2; GI consult; Consider transfusion
RATIONALE: Melena/bloody stool with dizziness suggests significant GI hemorrhage

LABEL: Syncope without palpitations
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: ECG; Orthostatic vitals; CBC; BMP; Consider Holter monitor
RATIONALE: Syncope with dizziness needs cardiac evaluation even without palpitations

LABEL: Appendicitis pattern
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: CT abdomen/pelvis with contrast; CBC; CRP; Surgical consult; NPO
RATIONALE: RLQ pain + fever strongly suggests appendicitis

LABEL: GI hemorrhage
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: CBC stat; Type and screen; IV access x2; GI consult; Consider transfusion; NPO
RATIONALE: GI bleeding requires emergent evaluation and possible intervention

LABEL: AAA warning
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: CT angiography abdomen stat; Emergent vascular surgery consult; Large bore IV x2; Type and crossmatch
RATIONALE: Hypotension + back pain suggests ruptured AAA

LABEL: Pancreatitis pattern
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: Lipase; CT abdomen; IV fluids; Pain management; NPO; GI consult
RATIONALE: Epigastric pain + back radiation + vomiting = classic pancreatitis

LABEL: Ectopic pregnancy
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: Quantitative hCG stat; Pelvic ultrasound; Large bore IV; Type and crossmatch; OB/GYN consult stat
RATIONALE: Pelvic pain + missed period + hemodynamic instability = ruptured ectopic until proven otherwise

LABEL: Mesenteric ischemia
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: CT angiography abdomen stat; Lactate; Surgical consult; IV heparin if confirmed; NPO
RATIONALE: AFib + postprandial pain + hemodynamic instability suggests mesenteric ischemia

LABEL: Cholecystitis pattern
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: RUQ ultrasound; CBC; LFTs; Lipase; Surgical consult; NPO
RATIONALE: RUQ pain + fever suggests cholecystitis requiring urgent evaluation

LABEL: Possible ectopic - stable
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: Quantitative hCG; Pelvic ultrasound; OB/GYN follow-up; Return precautions for bleeding/pain
RATIONALE: Pelvic pain + missed period needs ectopic evaluation even if hemodynamically stable
